title: Eglise Saint-Germain-des-Prés, Paris - TwoBoomersAbroad
highlights: King Childebert in the mid-6th century, Saint-Germain-des-Prés is the oldest church in Paris. Childebert was engaged in a war in Spain in the city of Saragossa when he heard that the citizens had placed themselves under the protection of the martyr St. Vincent. Childebert acquired St. Vincent's robe and took the relic back to France to be housed in the church which he named after the saint.
